estimands.description: Average post-event change in somatic_ambient_anxiety_mean among eligible US English online adult respondents, comparing the primary follow-up window with the primary baseline window.
Differential post-event change in somatic_ambient_anxiety_mean for respondents classified as exposed or high-exposure relative to a preregistered lower-exposure comparison group.
estimands.availability_rule: Permitted only if the locked registry and fielding data contain a defensible comparison definition before outcome inspection.

weighting.weighting_sensitive_rule: Label evidence weighting-sensitive if effective sample size is below 65 percent of nominal analytic N in either primary period.

model_formulas.interrupted_repeated_cross_section: somatic_ambient_anxiety_mean ~ post_event + age_group + gender + race_ethnicity + education + census_region + employment_status + household_income_band + political_ideology_or_party_id + prior_ai_use + ai_news_attention_30d + occupational_ai_exposure + brief_general_anxiety_or_distress + device_type + day_of_week + sample_source
model_formulas.difference_in_differences: somatic_ambient_anxiety_mean ~ post_event * exposed + age_group + gender + race_ethnicity + education + census_region + employment_status + household_income_band + political_ideology_or_party_id + prior_ai_use + ai_news_attention_30d + occupational_ai_exposure + brief_general_anxiety_or_distress + device_type + day_of_week + sample_source
model_formulas.event_time_bins: somatic_ambient_anxiety_mean ~ event_time_bin + age_group + gender + race_ethnicity + education + census_region + employment_status + household_income_band + political_ideology_or_party_id + prior_ai_use + ai_news_attention_30d + occupational_ai_exposure + brief_general_anxiety_or_distress + device_type + day_of_week + sample_source
model_formulas.placebo_pretrend: somatic_ambient_anxiety_mean ~ late_pre_period + age_group + gender + race_ethnicity + education + census_region + employment_status + household_income_band + political_ideology_or_party_id + prior_ai_use + ai_news_attention_30d + occupational_ai_exposure + brief_general_anxiety_or_distress + device_type + day_of_week + sample_source

standard_errors.cluster_rule: Cluster by respondent_id if a respondent can appear in both baseline and follow-up. Otherwise use heteroskedasticity-robust standard errors.

secondary_item_family.gate: Interpret only if the primary construct test passes.

heterogeneity_family.gate: Heterogeneity cannot rescue a failed primary construct test.

robustness_checks.description: Run the primary model without survey weights.
Run the primary model with weights trimmed to 0.20 and 5.00.
Exclude follow-up respondents who report no awareness of the event before survey completion.
Exclude respondents reporting a major unrelated personal crisis in the prior week.
Exclude respondents in the highest decile of pre-outcome brief general anxiety or distress.
Restrict follow-up to +24 hours through +3 days.
Compare day -14 through day -8 with day -7 through day -2.
Report primary estimates when event-day buffer interviews are included and when they are excluded.
Analyze the negative-control outcome if collected with stable wording across baseline and follow-up.

descriptive_only.condition: Any required N threshold, registry lock, confound, or primary model threshold fails.

blocked_claims: long-term effect beyond the follow-up window
country, language, offline, adolescent, clinical, organization, or occupation claims outside the sampled frame
item-level headline claim when the construct-level test fails
